root.description: The root node of the plot tree (leaf, grid, or compare).

mode.description: Which `DataSource` the bootstrap builds for `data` resolution across the whole tree. Optional — omitted (or "local") means the self-contained LOCAL store; kept optional so it stays in lockstep with the Python `PlotDescriptorSpec` default (`mode="local"`).

endpoint.description: ENDPOINT only: absolute base URL of the repo server (no trailing slash), used to build `${endpoint}/api/artifacts/${hash}`.

data.description: How to produce the renderer's DATA props.

PlotLeafNode.description: A single renderer + its data — the former flat descriptor body.

format.description: OPTIONAL format hint for the image blob (a MIME type or extension token, e.g. `"png"`, `"avif"`, `"npy"`). Drives the multi-format DECODER seam (`resolveDataProps`): when it names a RAW-buffer format (`npy`/`npz`), the bytes are fetched and normalized through `decodeImage` — float buffers → the `hdr` prop shape, uint8 buffers → an `imageUrl` data URL. Absent (or a browser-native format like png/jpeg/webp/avif/gif) keeps the byte-identical URL fast path — the browser decodes it via ``.

url.description: OPTIONAL direct URL to the image blob (additive to `hash`). When present, `resolveDataProps` FETCHES the bytes from this URL and normalizes them through `decodeImage` (sniffed by MIME/URL-ext/magic) — the same shaping as the `format?` path: float buffers → the `hdr` prop shape, uint8/native buffers → an `imageUrl` PNG data URL. This is the CLIENT-DECODE path that lets a URL serve formats the browser can't ``-decode (`exr`/`npy`/…), with the image referenced by URL instead of embedded in the HTML. (For a plain browser-native URL a bare-`str` `kind:"url"` passthrough is lighter; this field opts into the fetch+decode path.) NOTE: a cross-origin fetch is CORS-gated — the serving endpoint must allow the page's origin.

DataSpec.description:
How the renderer's DATA props are produced.

 - `inline`: the data-contract props are plain JSON, carried directly in the    descriptor (2D contracts — Series[]/points[]/matrix/counts+edges/table/    figure). The bootstrap merges `props` straight onto the renderer. No    `DataSource` needed.  - `image`: a content-addressed image artifact (+ optional baseline +    overlay metadata). Resolved via `resolveImageViewportItems` against the    active `DataSource` (LOCAL `data:` URL or ENDPOINT `/api/artifacts/…`),    yielding `{ imageUrl, baselineUrl, overlay }` for `ImagePane`.  - `npz`: a content-addressed 3D binary artifact (`.npy`/`.npz`) for the    three.js renderers (G3). `objectType` selects which 3D type the bytes    belong to (`pointcloud` is the only one wired in G3a; the others land in    G3b). `hash` keys the LOCAL store / ENDPOINT artifact; `meta` is the    Python-baked artifact metadata (channels/bounds/n_points/…) carried inline    so the renderer needs a SINGLE bytes fetch, no second metadata round trip.

GridNode.description: Children laid out in a CSS grid. `colWidths`/`rowHeights` entries: number → `Nfr`, string → verbatim CSS ("25%", "120px").

SharedProps.description: Properties shared across a grid's cells (colormap/range/colorbar/reference, plus opt-in viewport/camera sync).

baselineIndex.description: Which frame is the reference/baseline (0 = `a`, 1 = `b`). Default 0.

CompareNode.description: Two DataSpec frames composited into one pane (split/blend/diff).
